subject: [PATCH 3/6] fs/proc/task_mmu: fix hugetlb self-deadlock in pagemap_scan_pte_hole()

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch fixes a self-deadlock issue in the Linux kernel's procfs task_mmu module when handling huge page (hugetlb) memory mappings. The deadlock occurs because the pagemap_scan_pte_hole function, which is responsible for scanning and write-protecting unpopulated hugetlb entries, takes a read lock on the hugetlb VMA while also trying to take a write lock on the same VMA. This patch avoids this deadlock by directly installing uffd-wp markers under the page-table lock, rather than routing through the uffd_wp_range function. The fix is similar to how populated hugetlb entries are handled in pagemap_scan_hugetlb_entry. The patch also ensures that PMD sharing is disabled on uffd-wp VMAs, making it safe to skip taking the VMA write lock.
